body:
Fixes #102

## Summary
- Normalize Google Docs public-export HTML/Markdown in both JS and Rust `--capture api` flows so output matches the browser path more closely.
- Rebuild margin-based nested lists, group adjacent blockquote paragraphs, normalize tables, strip inherited heading emphasis, and clean Markdown artifacts such as escaped numeric heading dots and single-tilde strike markers.
- Preserve the reproduction data, before/after Markdown, raw public export HTML, extracted images, research notes, and verification logs under [`docs/case-studies/issue-102`](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/tree/issue-102-7f05baee1eea/docs/case-studies/issue-102).

## Reproduction
The public Google Doc from the issue was captured through the public-export API path. Before the fix, the API output showed escaped heading numbering, degraded strike formatting, flattened nested lists, split blockquotes, and padded/leaky tables. The captured before/after artifacts are committed in [`docs/case-studies/issue-102/reference`](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/tree/issue-102-7f05baee1eea/docs/case-studies/issue-102/reference).

## Verification
- `cd js && npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/unit/gdocs.test.js --runInBand`
- `cd js && npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/integration/gdocs-public-doc.test.js --runInBand`
- `cd js && GDOCS_INTEGRATION=1 npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/integration/gdocs-public-doc.test.js --runInBand --testNamePattern='fetches the public document via --capture api'`
- `cd js && npm run lint`
- `cd js && npm run format:check`
- `cd js && npm run check:duplication`
- `cd js && node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`
- `cd rust && cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cd rust && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cd rust && cargo test --test integration gdocs::test_public_export_markdown_normalization_issue_102 -- --nocapture`
- `cd rust && GDOCS_INTEGRATION=1 RUST_LOG=off cargo test --test integration gdocs_public_doc::live_capture_of_public_document_preserves_every_section -- --nocapture`
- `cd rust && cargo test`

Note: a full local JS `npm test -- --runInBand` was also attempted, but this environment does not have the Playwright Chromium binary installed and Docker-backed suites cannot start here. The focused JS unit/integration/API checks above pass; the CI workflow installs browser dependencies for the broader suite.
title: Fix Google Docs public-export Markdown fidelity
